combat.name: Combat
skills: +1 to all injury rolls caused in hand-to-hand combat.
Fighting 2+ enemies: +1 Attack each close-combat phase; immune to All Alone tests.
May use any hand-to-hand weapon, not just those in his equipment options.
+1 to all his Critical Hit rolls in hand-to-hand combat.

Re-roll all missed attacks in the close-combat phase of the turn he CHARGES, while armed with a sword (RB 122). FAQ: Only normal swords or weeping blades — not double-handed swords or any other weapon.
Each time he suffers a wound in close combat he may make an additional saving throw of 5+. This save is never modified and is taken after all other armour saves.

shooting.name: Shooting
skills: May shoot twice per turn with a bow or crossbow (not a crossbow pistol).
Brace of pistols: Fire twice; a single pistol may fire the turn it was reloaded.
+6" to the range of any missile weapon.
May use any missile weapon, not just those on his warband list.
May move and fire with weapons that normally require staying still (not with Quick Shot).
Ignores all cover modifiers when shooting.
May fire every turn with a handgun or Hochland long rifle.
Throw up to three throwing knives/stars per shooting phase, split between targets (not with Quick Shot).

academic.name: Academic
skills: Leader only: +6" to his Leader range (not Undead leaders).
Spellcasters only: +1 to rolls to cast spells (not Sisters of Sigmar / Warrior-Priests).
+2 to the roll to find rare items.
Deduct 2D6 gc from one item's price (min 1 gc), once per post-battle sequence.
May learn Lesser Magic if he owns a Tome of Magic (not Witch Hunters / Sisters / Warrior-Priests).
Re-roll one die on the Exploration chart (second result stands).
Spellcasters only: May wear armour and still cast spells.

strength.name: Strength
skills: +1 WS and +1 Attack when fighting inside buildings or ruins.
-1 Strength to all close-combat hits against him (does not affect armour save modifiers).
May use a double-handed weapon without the always-strikes-last penalty.

speed.name: Speed
skills: Leap D6" in the movement phase, in addition to normal movement (once per turn).
Triple Movement when running or charging (instead of doubling).
Fall/jump up to 12" unharmed on an Initiative test; re-roll failed Diving Charge rolls.
Strikes first against chargers (compare Initiative if both would strike first).

Springs up instantly if knocked down (counts as having moved). Ignores 'knocked down' results when rolling for injuries — except when knocked down by a successful helmet save or if he has the No Pain rule (FAQ).
Avoid any missile hit on a D6 roll of 5+ (rolled when hit, before rolling to wound).
Climb up or down twice his Movement with no Initiative tests.

restlessSkills.name: Restless Dead Special Skills
skills: Secretly nominate one Zombie as a Corpse Bomb. If it charges or is charged it detonates: all models within D6" take D3 Strength 4 hits, and the Zombie is destroyed. Corpse Bombs killed by shooting do not detonate. Only one at a time (Liche and Necromancer may both take this skill).
At the start of the battle deploy D3 Zombies for free. They do not count toward the maximum warband size (but add to rating), cannot be Corpse Bombs and last only for the battle.

Instead of his normal attacks, make one unarmed Wraith Touch: If it hits it wounds automatically. A Liche that wounds with it regains one lost wound (not above its starting total); Necromancers do not. No effect on the Possessed or Undead.
If the hero did not search for rare items in the last exploration phase, he starts the next battle with a pool of D3+1 modifiers (+1) he may add to his casting rolls, as many at a time as he wishes.
The maximum warband size is increased by 1.

ngSkills.name: Night Goblin Special Skills
ngSkills.note: Night Goblin Heroes (instead of any standard list).
skills: Adds +6" to the range of any missile weapon he uses (nets excluded).
Big Boss only. After all other deployment, he may move D3 of his warband members.
Set up after the opposing warband, anywhere out of sight and more than 12" from any enemy. If both sides infiltrate, roll D6 each — lowest sets up first.

'Chuck and charge': Declare a net charge and throw a net at the target. If it hits and the target fails to escape, the target counts as knocked down and the Goblin completes the charge; otherwise it is a failed charge (stop 1" away).

merchantSkills.name: Merchant Special Skills
merchantSkills.note: Merchant Caravans Merchant (instead of the standard lists).
skills: Whenever a Rout test is required, the Merchant may pay 5 gc per non-Hero member still in the game; if he does, one already-out-of-action member does not count for that Rout test. Repeatable while gold lasts.

If the Merchant was not taken out of action, before the trading phase he may take an Ld test: On a pass the warband gains 1 gc per Experience point the Merchant has; on a fail it loses up to that amount.
If not taken out of action, the Merchant may search for D3+1 rare items after each battle instead of one.
+1 on the roll to see what an item fetches when selling via the Trade special rule.
Instead of searching for rare items, the Merchant may visit the black market for special items (Dispel Scroll, Lesser Artefact, Magical Artefact, Magical Scroll); such items can never be sold back.

ogreSkills.name: Ogres Special Skills
ogreSkills.note: Maneaters Heroes (instead of the standard lists).
skills: The Ogre may wield a Difficult to Use weapon and a hand weapon at the same time (but not two Difficult to Use weapons).
May unleash thunderous fumes on all enemies engaged with him: those that fail an Ld test suffer −1 to hit for the turn. He must wait until a new enemy engages him before doing it again.
Immediately learn one skill from the Shooting or Academic lists (once only; not available to the Mountain Guide).
When charging, may make a single attack at +1 to hit instead of his normal attacks; if it hits, the target is automatically knocked down.
Leader only. The warband may hire Hired Swords available to Mercenaries; if the leader dies, all such Hired Swords are removed.
Leader only. Re-roll the first failed Rout test of each battle.

cavalcadeSkills.name: Dark Elf Special Skills (Fury of Khaine)
cavalcadeSkills.note: Dark Elf Heroes may use these skills instead of any of the standard skill tables available to them.
skills: A whirlwind in hand-to-hand: If he takes ALL of his current opponents Out Of Action he may make a 4" follow-up move. If this brings him into contact with a new enemy a new combat starts next turn, counting as a charge. May not follow up in the opponent's turn.

Strongly built for an Elf — the warrior may also choose skills from the Strength table. The Sorceress may never take this skill, and no more than two warriors in the warband may have it at any one time.

Inhuman agility: May avoid a melee or missile attack on a D6 roll of 6. Combined with Step Aside or Dodge this improves to 4+ in the relevant area (e.g. Fey Quickness + Step Aside = avoid melee on 4+).

Set up after the opposing warband, anywhere on the table that is out of sight of the enemy and more than 12" from any enemy model. If both players have infiltrators, roll a D6 each — lowest sets up first.

If the Hero does not search for rare items after a battle, he may instead brew D3-1 doses of Dark Venom (may yield none). The poison must be used in the next battle and can never be sold or traded to other warbands.

chapelKnightSkills.name: Knightly Skills
chapelKnightSkills.note: Bretonnian Chapel Guard Knights (instead of the standard tables).
skills: The Knight may learn one Virtue from the Bretonnian Knight Virtues list. Renowned Virtue may only be taken once.
Questing Knight only. When charging, charged by, or in combat with a fear-causing enemy, he may re-roll any one Leadership test (including Rout tests), accepting the second result.
Each turn the Knight may make one extra attack with a shield or kite shield, made at −1 Strength and treated as a club.
The Knight keeps the +1/+2 Strength bonus from Flails and Morning Stars after the first round of combat.

Ignores movement penalties for wearing armour while on foot. Strength, axes, critical hits and similar modifiers cannot reduce his armour save below 5+, nor can it be ignored by any non-magical means.

battleMonkSkills.name: Battle Monk Skills
battleMonkSkills.note: Emissary & Dragon Monks (instead of a standard table). The Emissary may only ever pick Warmonger.
skills: If fighting unarmed, the Hero may reduce his Attacks by −1 to gain +1 Strength in close combat. He may sacrifice any number of attacks this way.
The monk may triple his Movement when running or charging, and may run even when there are enemy models within 8".
Cannot be intercepted while charging. He may escape from combat by leaping away without an Ld test and may declare a leaping charge in the same turn.

If two or more models are in close combat with the monk, he may pass an Initiative test (after the first attacks, before the second) to grab the first model as a shield — the second model's attacks are then directed at that grabbed model instead.
The Emissary may take an Ld test before the battle; if passed, D3+1 Raging Peasants join for that game (may exceed the maximum). Each Peasant is then subject to Hatred for the battle.

blackDwarfSkills.name: Chaos Dwarf Skills (Black Dwarfs)
blackDwarfSkills.note: Sorcerer & Gaolers (instead of one of the standard tables).
skills: After a game in which he was taken out of action, the Hero may re-roll once on the Serious Injury chart; the second result stands even if worse.
+3 when searching for Chaos Armour (incl. Mechanical Suits) or Obsidian Weapons, which he can craft himself; he ignores the Rarity and Gift of Chaos special rules. He may never wear Chaos Armour.
3+ save on a D6 to avoid being stunned (a stun becomes knocked down). With a helmet this is 2+ and replaces the normal helmet rule.
When rolling on the Exploration chart at the end of a game, the Hero may modify one dice by +1/−1.

Leader only. The leader may re-roll a failed Rout test (as long as he is not knocked down or stunned); the new result stands even if worse. If the leader is taken out of action the warband must make an immediate Rout test.
When rolling on the Injury table for this Hero, treat 1-3 as knocked down, 4-5 as stunned and only 6 as out of action.

chaosDwarfSkills.name: Chaos Dwarf Skills
chaosDwarfSkills.note: Apprentice Sorcerer, Champions & Bull Centaur (instead of one of the standard tables).
skills: When rolling on the Injury table for this warrior, treat 1-3 as knocked down, 4-5 as stunned and only 6 as out of action.
After the battle the warrior may re-roll one of his Serious Injury results; the second result stands.
The warrior Hates all enemies (re-roll missed to-hit rolls in the first turn of every close combat).
3+ save on a D6 to avoid being stunned (a stun becomes knocked down instead). With a helmet this save is 2+ and replaces the normal helmet rule.

amazonSkills.name: Amazon Skills
amazonSkills.note: Amazon Heroines (instead of the standard skill tables).
skills: Always strikes first in the first close-combat round against Skinks (regardless of who charged).
May re-roll one out-of-action roll after the battle (the second result stands).
Enemy in base contact: Ld test at the start of each turn, otherwise it cannot attack (defence only). Useless against Lizardmen/Undead.
+1 A when charging; immune to charm effects and fear.
Hidden in jungle terrain: Enemies halve the distance at which they can spot her.

tgSkills.name: Tomb Guardian Additional Skills
skills: Chariots are very difficult to control and a warrior must have this skill to drive a chariot effectively in combat. A charioteer without this skill cannot charge. (Mordheimer inconsistency note: The skill is classified Academic, which the Tomb Lord cannot normally access.)

pitSkills.name: Pit Fighter Skills
pitSkills.note: Pit Fighter Heroes (instead of the standard tables).
skills: Ignores the heavy-weapon penalty entirely — the Strength bonus (e.g. morning star) applies in every round.
On going OOA: Toughness test (−1 each subsequent round). On success he stands up and fights on (a 2nd OOA is final).
Ignores all "difficult to use" rules — may e.g. combine a morning star + buckler, or two morning stars.
Instead of a normal charge, a shoulder slam: one attack at +1 S, +1 to hit, no weapon bonuses, criticals on 5+.

cavalrySkills.name: Riding / Cavalry Skills
cavalrySkills.note: Mounted warriors (e.g. Imperial Outriders) with the Ride skill.
skills: Mounted leader: Leadership radius +6" (in addition to other bonuses).
Shooting against him −1; requires an Initiative test (otherwise loss of control). Not with heavy armour/shield/missile weapons.
When charging an unmounted enemy: one extra S4 attack.
Always strikes first in close combat against dismounted enemies.
Ride up to the mount's movement, then dismount at once (counts as a diving charge from 2").
Mount within 2" and immediately make a full move/charge.
Shoots at full gallop and in a 360° arc (additional −1 to hit).
Against mounted enemies: A wounded enemy model gets +1 on its "Whoa Boy!" table.

dthSkills.name: Dwarf Special Skills
dthSkills.note: Dwarf Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: When using a weapon with the Parry rule he parries on an equal or higher roll (not just higher). With two parrying weapons he may parry up to two attacks; with two Dwarf axes he may re-roll failed parries.
When rolling on the Heroes' Serious Injury chart after being taken out of action, the dice may be re-rolled once (the second result must be accepted, even if worse).
When rolling on the Exploration chart at the end of a game, he may modify one dice by +1/−1.
When rolling on the Injury table for this Hero, 1–3 = knocked down, 4–5 = stunned, 6 = out of action.
3+ save on a D6 to avoid being stunned (treat as knocked down instead). With a helmet this save is 2+ (replaces the normal helmet rule).

arabSkills.name: Arab Special Skills
arabSkills.note: Arab Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: Can bury himself in sand and become almost undetectable: may hide in open ground. Cannot be used inside buildings.
May run and shoot, but suffers −2 to hit instead of the −1 to hit for moving.
Weather conditions such as heat no longer affect this model.

bretSkills.name: Bretonnian Special Skills (Virtues)
bretSkills.note: Bretonnian Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: His purity of heart resists enemy magic: any spell cast against the Knight is dispelled on a D6 roll of 4+.
Against a model with a higher Strength than himself, he may re-roll any failed to-hit rolls in close combat.
Once per game, if he is not out of action, stunned or knocked down, you may re-roll a failed Rout test.
Contempt for dishonourable weapons: subject to Hatred of all enemies armed with shooting weapons.
Eager to get to grips: May add +D3" to his move when charging (roll each time before moving).

lizardSkinkSkills.name: Lizardmen Skills (Skinks)
lizardSkinkSkills.note: Skink Heroes may use this list instead of a standard list.
skills: Expert at sneaking up on his prey: may set up anywhere on the table but no closer than 12" to an enemy, and must start the game in hiding.
Adept at making the most of cover: an additional −1 to hit the Skink while he is in cover (i.e. −2 in total).

lizardSaurusSkills.name: Lizardmen Skills (Saurus)
lizardSaurusSkills.note: Saurus Heroes may use this list instead of a standard list.
skills: His roar is so deafening that enemy models in base contact suffer −1 to hit in the first round of combat against him.
Years of battle have hardened his hide: he is only taken out of action on a 6+.

deSkills.name: Dark Elf Special Skills
deSkills.note: Dark Elf Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: May make a 4" follow-up move if he takes all of his opponents out of action; contact with another enemy starts a new combat next turn (counts as charging). May not follow up in the opponent's turn.
May choose skills from the Strength table from now on. The Sorceress may never take this skill; no more than two warriors in the warband may have it at one time.
Avoids melee or missile attacks on a roll of 6; with Step Aside or Dodge this improves to 4+ in the relevant area.
Exactly the same as the Skaven skill.
If the Hero doesn't search for rare items, he may make D3−1 doses of Dark Venom instead (must be used in the next battle; cannot be sold or traded).

weSkills.name: Wood Elf Special Skills
weSkills.note: Wood Elf Heroes with the Special skill list may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: An understanding of magic: 4+ saving throw against hostile magic.
The Elven gods favour him: Once per game he may re-roll any failed roll.
Can spot hidden enemy models up to 2× his Initiative in inches away.
Expert tracker: May modify the result of one exploration die by +/−1. Only one Elven Hero may possess this skill.
Always deployed last, anywhere out of sight of the enemy. If both players infiltrate, roll D6 — lowest deploys first.

skavenSkills.name: Skaven Special Skills
skavenSkills.note: Skaven Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: Declare at the start of his turn: +1 attack and +D3" total move for the duration of his own turn, but suffers D3 S3 hits with no armour save at the end of the turn.
May wield a shield, knife or sword with his tail: an extra attack with the appropriate weapon, or a +1 bonus to his armour save.
Needs no Initiative test when climbing walls and other sheer surfaces.
Always placed after the opposing warband, anywhere out of sight and more than 12" from any enemy. If both players infiltrate, roll D6 — lowest sets up first.
Fights with bare paws without penalties and counts as having two weapons (+1 attack); causes critical hits on a To Wound roll of 5–6. With Fighting Claws: +2 attacks instead of +1.

pestilensSkills.name: Clan Pestilens Special Skills
pestilensSkills.note: Members of Clan Pestilens may choose these skills instead of the standard ones.
skills: As the Skaven skill: +1 attack and +D3" move for his turn; D3 S3 hits (no save) at the end of the turn.
Requires Black Hunger. Gains Frenzy; the only weapon he may use in close combat is a censer.
Immune to poisons and diseases; if taken out of combat by a failed censer test he does not roll for injuries (recovers automatically).

Requires Rotten Body. A model that takes the bearer out of action in close combat must take a Toughness test (D6): if the roll is higher than its Toughness it suffers an automatic wound (a 6 always wounds). Undead/Possessed never test.
Requires the Resilient skill. Treats Stunned injuries as Knocked Down.

slayerSkills.name: Troll Slayer Skills
slayerSkills.note: Dwarf Slayers may use this list as well as any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: May double his attacks in the turn he charges; suffers −1 to hit that turn.
Always wounds any opponent on a roll of 4+, regardless of Toughness (unless his own Strength would mean a lower roll is needed).
+1 to his close-combat to-hit rolls during the turn he charges.

pirateSkills.name: Pirate Special Skills
pirateSkills.note: Pirate Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: At the start of his close-combat phase he may burst into song: one opponent in base contact must pass a Ld test or lose 1 Attack that turn. No effect on Undead or other non-living creatures (e.g. Possessed).
If he Falls, he may ignore the D3 hits on a single roll of 4+. If knocked down or stunned within 1" of a precipice, he may re-roll the Initiative test to avoid falling.
With a Sword he parries already on an equal roll (not just higher) — but only when not in the open (in cover, in a building, or within 2" of a terrain feature).
Captain only: Once per turn, one pirate within 8" who failed a test to flee combat (or to stop fleeing) may re-roll it. The Captain must be on his feet and not in close combat himself.
May ignore any Critical Hit on a roll of 5+ (the wound is then treated as normal).
At the end of any close-combat phase while in base contact with enemies, he may take a Ld test: If passed, he makes a normal move away (no run/charge) without the enemy striking blows.

norseSkills.name: Norse Special Skills
norseSkills.note: Norse Heroes with the Special skill list may use this list instead of the normal ones.
skills: When armed with a shield he may parry with it in addition to getting the 6+ save.
When armed with an axe or double-handed weapon, he may re-roll all failed to-hit rolls when he charges.
No enemy may parry an attack made by this hero — it strikes with such power that it pushes right through a buckler or sword.
Leader only: Models within 12" may use his Leadership (instead of the normal 6").
Never needs to take All Alone tests and may re-roll failed Fear tests.

swSkills.name: Shadow Warrior Special Skills
swSkills.note: Shadow Warrior Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: Exactly the same as the Skaven skill: Always placed after the opposing warband, anywhere out of sight and more than 12" from any enemy. If both players infiltrate, roll D6 — lowest sets up first.
May always roll to charge opponents he cannot see (instead of the normal 4"), as long as he has the movement to reach them.
An enemy attempting to detect him while he is Hidden must halve its Initiative before measuring the distance.
If Hidden, may shoot or cast spells and remain Hidden. If the target is not taken out of action, it tests against its Initiative — on a success the Sniper is spotted and no longer hidden.
May choose skills from the Strength table from now on. Not for Shadow Weavers; only two members of the warband may ever have this skill at one time.

Shadow Weaver only: +1 to his Dispel roll with Elven Runestones. May also inscribe one warrior's gear with runes: that warrior may re-roll a single failed armour save or Parry once per battle (must be redone after each battle).

sosSkills.name: Sisters of Sigmar Special Skills
sosSkills.note: Sisters of Sigmar may use this list instead of the standard skill lists.
skills: Possessed or Undead opponents lose their first attack against the Sister in the first round of close combat (down to a minimum of 1).
Any spell which would affect her is nullified on a D6 roll of 4+. A nullified spell does not affect any other models either.
Matriarch only: May re-roll any failed Rout tests.
Hates all Skaven, Undead, Possessed, Beastmen, Dark Elves and any other evil warband (and all models in them). Non-Chaos humans who merely kill and loot are not included.
May re-roll any Fear tests and does not have to test if fighting alone against several opponents.

ostlanderSkills.name: Ostlander Special Skills
ostlanderSkills.note: Ostlander Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: When charging, may attempt to knock down the opponent instead of his normal attacks: one to-hit roll at +1; no wound roll — on a hit the opposing model is Knocked Down.
Reeks of alcohol: All living enemies (not Undead/Possessed) are at −1 to hit him in close combat. May not carry open flames; fire attacks against him are at +1 Strength.

In the shooting phase, instead of shooting/casting: one visible enemy (LOS rules as shooting) takes a Ld test — if failed it must spend its next movement phase trying to get into close combat with the taunter.
'Normal' animals never attack him; up to two wardogs he owns do not count against the warband's maximum size.
Leader only: May re-roll a single Rout test once per game.

beastmenSkills.name: Beastmen Special Skills
beastmenSkills.note: Beastmen Heroes may use this list instead of the standard skill lists available to them.
skills: The Beastman's massively shaggy hide acts as armour: 6+ armour save that can be combined with other armour as normal.
The Beastman may buy one mutation (see the Possessed mutations).
Immune to fear, terror and All Alone tests.
Mighty horns: May make an additional attack with his basic Strength in a turn he charges.
Beastman Chief only: May re-roll any failed Rout tests.
Affected by the rules of hatred when fighting any Human warbands.

orcSkills.name: Orc Special Skills
orcSkills.note: Orc Heroes (Orc Mob) may use this list instead of any standard list available to them.
skills: Special 3+ save on a D6 to avoid being stunned (treat as knocked down instead). With a helmet this save is 2+ (replaces the normal helmet rule).
Expert at bulldozing charges: May add +D3" to his charge range.
May ignore Fear and Terror tests when charging.
Boss only. The warband may re-roll any failed Rout test as long as the Boss is not out of action.
Thick, dark-green skin (possibly Black Orc blood): may add +1 to any armour saves.
Massive strength, aims at heads: any knocked down results the Orc causes in close combat count as stunned instead.

blackOrcSkills.name: Black Orc Special Skills
blackOrcSkills.note: Black Orc Heroes may use this list instead of the standard skill lists available to them.
skills: Young'un with the Black Orc blood ability and 25 experience only. He now counts as a full Black Orc Warrior: Follows all Black Orc rules, uses their equipment list and skill lists.
Special 3+ save on a D6 to avoid being stunned (treat as knocked down instead). With a helmet this save is 2+ (replaces the normal helmet rule).
May add +D3" to his charge range.
May ignore Fear and Terror tests when charging.
Boss only. The warband may re-roll any failed Rout test as long as the Boss is not out of action.
Any knocked down results the Orc causes in close combat count as stunned instead.

hornedHunterSkills.name: Horned Hunter Special Skills
hornedHunterSkills.note: Horned Hunter Heroes may use this list instead of any standard list available to them.
skills: Masterful trap setter: Trip wire range extends to 4"; traps set by him trigger on 2+.
Always placed after the opposing warband, anywhere out of sight and more than 12" from any enemy. If both players infiltrate, roll D6 — lowest sets up first.
Reeks of alcohol and sweat: All living enemies (not Undead/Possessed) are at −1 to hit him in close combat. He may not carry open flames; fire attacks against him are at +1 Strength.
'Normal' animals (warhorses, warhounds, etc.) will never attack him; up to two wardogs he owns do not count against the warband's maximum size.
Roll one additional D6 during the exploration phase. A warband may only contain one pathfinder.
Crouches unseen in any cover: Enemies must halve their Initiative when attempting to find him while he is Hidden.

marauderSkills.name: Marauder Skills
marauderSkills.note: Marauders of Chaos Heroes (instead of the standard skill tables).
skills: The Hero counts as a Chaos Warrior: Uses the maximum profile for Warriors of Chaos (M4 WS8 BS8 S5 T5 W3 I8 A5 Ld9) and the Hero equipment list.
Leader only. The "Eye of the Gods" effect (Spawn/Mark) occurs already on 10+ (Norse leaders 11+).
If the Hero takes an enemy OOA with a double-handed weapon, he may immediately make an additional attack against another model in base contact. Requires Strongman.
The Hero buys a mutation (see Possessed mutations). May be taken more than once. With the Mark of Onogal, a Blessing of Nurgle instead (except the Mark of Nurgle).
Leader only. May re-roll failed Rout tests and is immune to fear and all-alone tests.

banditSkills.name: Bandit Special Skills
banditSkills.note: Hochland Bandit Heroes may use this list instead of any standard skill tables available to them.
skills: Instead of searching for rare equipment between games, roll a D6: 2-6 = D6+1 gold to the stash; on a 1 the robbery goes wrong (roll on the Serious Injury table as if taken Out of Action). Cannot be combined with the Huckster's Slick Operator.
An enemy trying to detect this warrior while he is Hidden must halve his Initiative (round up) before measuring the spotting distance.

If in close combat (and not knocked down/stunned) at the start of his Movement phase, the warrior may make an Initiative test to leave combat: success moves him 1" away (free) and he may then act normally; failure means he strikes last that round.
While Hiding, the warrior may shoot or cast spells and still remain Hidden — not when firing a black-powder weapon.
If an enemy tries to detect the Hidden bandit, roll a D6: on 4+ he stays undetected and Hidden.
